Originally agreed on handshake deal with Seth for $2000 to go to trial. Lawyer was notified of my plea deal in the early May of 2025, plea deal was not made known to me until the day of my Jury Trial announcement, felt very pressured and bumrushed on day of JTA to take the deal, afterwards lawyer told me he would’ve like to go to trial and that we had a chance. Walked away from the whole experience like I was just young person being taken advantage of for not knowing the ins and outs of the justice system, after having multiple consultations with other lawyers in the Denton area I have been told while it does seem to be nefarious, I basically have no recourse. I take accountability for not being more level headed in the moment, not standing my ground, and not doing my homework. I know we all gotta make a living somehow, but I only wish to let other people know my experience with this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jason, I'm so sorry this is how you felt. I was ready and eager to go to trial. I would NEVER pressure a client into taking a deal.My reputation is CLEARLY the opposite, where I am accused by prosecutors of pressuring my clients INTO going to trial. That reputation is why your attorney of record brought me on.  But I do understand that the plea trap system can be very hectic and stressful on clients and in the future I will do my best to communicate offers earlier. However, since you felt it appropriate to give me a bad review, I think it appropriate to respond that publicly available records show that you got a deal that most people would be extremely happy with. It's so crazy to me that several of my one-star reviews are from people who got a reduction or dismissal of their charges.

reviews.text: Absolutely amazing . Seth represented my minor son , his staff communicated efficiently . The coordinated as a team throughout the entire process . Couldn’t have been blessed with a better attorney !! Thank you all !!!

Seth was an amazing attorney. Got straight to the point and settled everything for me. He's a true trial attorney and did not back down or pressure me to settle my case. Claudia answered all the questions or concerns I had was also a delight to talk too. HIGHLY RECOMMEND! Thank you Seth and Claudia for everything!

This dude is the worst. He wholesale didn't even know he was assigned to my roommate's case for almost 2 weeks, lied to me the entire time about filing a motion for a PR bond, didn't file the order needed and then continued to lie to my face about the fact that he had a JUDGE PERSONALLY TEXTING HIM that he sent over the necessary forms for my roommate to be released. he was in for a traffic ticket yall... the officer was already messed up for arresting him on a ticket, and then he is gonna wind up in there over Christmas bc Seth Fuller is completely incapable of accountability and will not tell the truth.

MAYBE HE SHOULD BE READING UP ON THE CASE FILES HE'S BACKLOGGED ON RATHER THAN FIGHTING WITH STRANGERS ON THE INTERNET..... JUST SAYING! I hope you have the christmas you deserve sir.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hey Brandi, Seth here. I never represented you, I do not handle traffic tickets and I am not a liar. I am a rigorously honest man and if I do not reveal client matters on the internet, so if I said I was texting with somebody, I was texting with somebody and would gladly provide proof to any actual client. It seems to me you are angry that my office closed at noon on the Friday before Christmas and won't answer your multiple, repeated phone calls. I have instructed my office to not answer them any more, as you are not my client and we do not tolerate harassing behavior from anyone, including clients. I will tell you that i would NEVER leave a client in jail for Christmas or any other day if it was in my power otherwise. I hope you and your roommate have a wonderful Christmas at home.

reviews.text: 2nd worst lawyer I’m the DFW the worst one Retired. Seth Fuller is has horrible customer service and doesn’t listening his clients. He will get you convicted. reviews.ownerResponse: While I would never discuss any specific client's case, what I CAN say is that I've won my last 7 adult criminal trials IN A ROW, including one just two weeks before this writing. In fact, in my last 11 adult criminal trials, 9 ended in full Not Guilty acquittals on all counts, one ended in a mistrial, and one ended in a guilty on a lesser included misdemeanor (not guilty on the felony). When you break them down into individual  counts, I'm 11-1-1. If that makes me the second worse attorney, I'd like to see the trial records of the top ten.

reviews.text: Horrible law firm . He doesn’t listen to your wishes. He will lie to you and accepts motions that go against your interests. I had to file a grievance against Seth Fuller because he lied to me. He then lied in court documents and stated I was getting a paid attorney. He was let go because I filed a grievance against him with the State Bar for going against my best interests. When I caught him in a lie he said it was strategy. I’m a disabled veteran and he took advantage of me. Please don’t spend your money on a low quality lawyer he doesn’t listen to his clients. If he is your appointed lawyer record every conversation
reviews.ownerResponse: I can only recall one criminal law client who actually hired me and was unhappy, but that was because he paid me $500 and stopped paying so I was forced to withdraw, That was only the second time in 14 years I'd had to "fire" a criminal client. He then fired his two subsequent court appointed attorneys and is set to represent himself in jury trial. The one I "fired" before that was actually satisfied with me but also didn't come up with the retainer he thought he could. He went through 8 subsequent attorneys before representing himself at trial, receiving 60 years from the jury. As for the rest, see my reply to the review you left from your other account.

reviews.text: Jackie and team deliver excellent service and peace of mind.  Navigating legal issues is never easy.  Jackie takes the time to share relevant details and possible outcomes in a way I can understand without being overwhelming. I know my family’s best interests are always her number one priority.

Except for the onboarding call, the team managing my case has been extremely difficult to get ahold of unless they were reaching out to me for bills and the retainer fee. (I will say they are a little pricier than other firms but worth every penny)

Beware this place firm will take your money. Hired them specifically Malorie when I did the consultation I was told it’s a fairly easy divorce. Straight forward she told me in cases like mine it would usually take 10-15k. So I hired the firm within 1 month retainer was gone. Within 2.5 months I was in already 15k. Only progress to that point was filing a response after being served. We didn’t even get to scheduling mediation. You will pay 2x for every talk she has with anyone. 1 charge to meet with her legal assistant to pass notes then 1 charge for the legal assistant to receive the note. With it being a bigger firm they have to log certain amount of hours to your case even if there is no updates. If you refuse to continue to fund the waiting they will drop you as a client. Money hungry firm!! They dropped me when I refused to reload my retainer making my total to 17k. I wasted 15k for Malorie Crosley to file a response. The rest went to meeting with legal assistant and weekly case review. Choose wisely don’t believe anything being told in the consultation. They are salesmen and sharks.
reviews.ownerResponse: We take client feedback seriously and always strive to provide the highest level of service and advocacy. While we are limited in what we can say due to attorney-client privilege and disagree with much of what you have posted regarding our representation of you and the amounts you paid us, we can confirm that we work diligently to guide our clients through their legal matters with transparency and professionalism. Every case is unique, and outcomes can be influenced by various factors, including the complexity of the situation and client participation. We understand that legal proceedings can be stressful, and we regret that you were not satisfied with your experience. Our goal is always to provide honest, strategic counsel while maintaining the integrity of the legal process. If there are any outstanding concerns, we encourage reaching out to our office directly to discuss them further. Thank you.

reviews.text: Extremely professional, and helpful! When calling to set up a consultation I was connected and had a date right away. I chose to work with Coker, Robb & Cannon because I knew they would look after everything. A special Thank you to Steven Vasquez who updated me throughout each step and made the process very simple!

I have been going back and forth with leaving this rating but honestly people should know that if I could leave a ZERO star rating, I would. This place was a terrible experience for me dealing with child custody that I had to change lawyers mid court case. The things I specifically wanted to be heard or addressed, were not. I also feel like my feelings and thoughts were just rejected and shut down by my lawyer Sheba each time we had spoken about my case. I had a mere 10 page binder during my court hearing while the other party had hundreds of pages. I sat there in court looking like a terrible person with no counter argument or defense. EVERYTHING was a mess and it seemed like my lawyer had NO idea what she talking about. Even after going through my whole invoice and writing down all the questioning bills like I was told by Kelly Robb (who was my first initial consult, but then was given Sheba) I was still billed SO MUCH and still paying it off. reviews.ownerResponse: Pamela, thank you for your feedback. Your perception is very important, though, and, if you would like to discuss your experience further, please reach out to our firm to schedule a call with our Founding Shareholder, Mr. Coker. reviews.text: Save yourself the time and don't use this law firm.  All they care about is making sure they get paid. reviews.text:
Hiring Youngberg Law Firm is the biggest mistake you will make.

I have over 15 reasons why you should not hire this firm, but that is not any fun to read. Basically, Youngberg embarrassed me and my family in the courts.

Youngberg was not familiar with the facts of the case.

Youngberg was not prepared for court.

Youngberg called their client by the wrong name in court.

Youngberg was unable to effectively cross examine testimony.

Youngberg did not present evidence with legal rules and allowed all evidence to be admitted against their client in the same manner.

Youngberg failed to meet the terms of their agreement. The involved state and city personnel were not presented as testifying witnesses to corroborate their case.

Youngberg did not present  case with the level of expertise I hired for legal representation.

Youngberg did not use my time wisely (or my money).

Youngberg created issues that caused delay, blamed someone else, and then charged me for it!

Youngberg sent a lot of pointless and purposeless emails. I learned to never trust an attorney that sends emails all day. They are not good attorneys -- they're great paper pushers. Which is needed! BUT, NOT WHAT I HIRED!

Youngberg wanted to be a therapist even when I told them it is not necessary, as opposed to being my legal representation.

Youngberg blamed the courts in an effort to avoid accepting responsibility for not meeting the terms and conditions of our agreement.

Youngberg was severely unprepared for court and did not have a strong, skilled courtroom presence.

Youngberg was offensive and devalued my family and myself when I expressed dissatisfaction with their representation, making issues more personal then they really needed to be. Very unprofessional!

BEWARE of this firm.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
This post has been updated to respond directly to MR YOUNGBERG on May 16, 2021.

Mr. Youngberg:

Your offices received an email outlining my dissatisfaction the night of the trial. The Court made a decision 10 days after my email. Therefore it is impossible that I am upset with the Court's decision.  You are gaslighting the situation and in my opinion disrespecting the State of Texas, in addition to your misrepresenting me.  Disgusting. I didn’t hire Cari Jacoby. I hired Youngberg.  Like I said at the top, it’s the WORST decision made.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Rebecca,

I am sorry to see your post and the description of your experience with my office. I do not agree with your review, or what you say happened during your time with my office. Additionally, your post is not reflective of the dozens of satisfied clients we represent each year or many we have represented since we began our practice in 2019.

I know that Cari Jacoby, your attorney, worked hard on your case and was prepared for your hearing and presented your case well. I know it is difficult when you don't get everything you wanted from the court. While all of our staff works hard to get our clients everything they are hoping for, ultimately, the judge is the one who makes the final decision when you have a court hearing. I hope that in the end you are able to resolve your case in a satisfactory manner as that is always our goal here at Youngberg Law Firm.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback.  Our office receives many inquiries, and to evaluate potential cases we need some basic details about the events involved. I am sorry you felt dismissed—that certainly was not my intent. reviews.text:
How an attorney treats the opposing side speaks greatly to their ethics.
After a confidential settlement agreement, attorney Brian Cartwright published personal private information about my past to a third-party in a disgusting attempt to discredit me.
I don’t think the specifics matter so much as the fact that he does not care about confidentiality agreements. The attorney should be bound to a higher level of responsibility and confidentiality because of their licensing. Attorney Cartwright sees no issue publishing private information to third parties after a confidential settlement agreement.
So if you thought your business would remain private, you’re in for a shock when he in fact will publish your business to third parties.
reviews.ownerResponse: The person posting this review was the opposing party in a case I was involved in.  The claims she makes against me in her post are incorrect.  She voluntarily signed an agreement that does not include any confidentiality clause.  It does, however, include a release of all claims against my clients and an express clause barring her from making defamatory statements.  Unfortunately, she chose to violate that agreement by posting false allegations online, which led a third party to publish her own defamatory comments.  Under the Texas Defamation Mitigation Act, I was required to send both of them formal requests for retraction.  Texas law required the requests to specify the circumstances causing various statements made to be defamatory.  In short, the language this person is now complaining about being disclosed to the third party was legally required — not optional — and was sent only to the third party for the sole purpose of complying with Texas law.  I have represented my clients ethically, legally, and vigorously, and I will continue to do so.

Tony (John) Ross was extremely committed and professional with how he dedicated himself to my case.  My charge was aggravated felony assault with bodily injury.  When you’re looking at 2 to 20 years in prison you can’t make a mistake choosing legal counsel.  I spent 36 days in jail and went through 3 different lawyer before getting out of jail.  Bad advice from legal counsel put me in jail and had my bond set at $200,000 and I was considered a flight risk.  I’m an educated man who has traveled across the US, Central America and done time in the military but I’ve never seen anything that compares to some of what happened to me at the hands of poor legal counsel.  Once I was out of jail I looked for and interviewed multiple lawyers, trying to find the one that was truly a good fit for me and was going to fight for me.  Tony sat with me listening intently as I told him the situation I was in and how everything had transpired.  When I was done Tony went through my story, asking question and piecing everything together from a legal stand point.  Tony told me he would represent me but he wanted me to be comfortable with him and how he approaches things.  Tony didn’t tell me “Hey I can get you off” and “You’re going to beat this”.  He told me I had a serious charge and it was an uphill battle but, from what I had said, it sounded like a self-defense situation.  Tony doesn’t promise you the world, take your money then leave you hanging.  He supports you and puts your mind at ease as you go through the process.  The best offer I had after 2 years was 8 years in prison.   After 2 years of going through the process losing my job, when I was in jail, then getting a better job with another company, the trial finally came.  After watching Tony in the court room and how he conducts himself, I knew I had chosen the right “Lawyer”.  Tony is calm, under control, intelligent, processes information quickly, pays attention to details and asks follow up questions when he knows the truth is not being told.  If you’re looking for courtroom theatrics, Tony’s not the person for you.  Regardless of the prosecutor’s demeanor and emotions Tony remains clam and professional.  After a 3 day trial it all ended with a hopelessly hung jury at 7 to 5.  After interviewing the jury it was found that the jury did not think the “victim” and his witness were believable. The jury attributed this to the questioning and how Tony had took apart their stories.  These were not 2 guys with criminal records either.  Bear in mind I never denied sticking this individual nor defending myself with zero witnesses to corroborate my story.  In the end the prosecution offered 8 years differed probation, community service.  In 250 cases seen by this judge he had never had a jury deliberate for 10 hours and come back hopelessly hung in a case like this.  I attribute all of the positive thing that happened in my case over 2 years, to “My Lawyer” Tony Ross.
1. My ability to walk away with an admission of non-guilt.  No contest
2. My 8 year differed probation. community service with no felony and absolutely nothing on my records
I walked away with my head held high and my reputation intact which was more important to me than anything else.  The day Tony told me to interview other lawyers and make sure I was comfortable with him was the day I knew Tony was the lawyer for me.  Yes Tony Ross is a lawyer, and he’s exceptional at it.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thanks, Chris. It was a pleasure getting to know you, and representing you throughout this ordeal. You know I'm always here if you need anything at all.
- Tony

Dear sir,
In an abundance of caution, I am not at liberty to respond in a point-by-point fashion in this forum, but suffice it to say, I do not believe that your post presents a fair, accurate or complete picture of the events. As you expressed to me, you did not want to go to trial as the offer eventually made to us was a VERY GOOD one. I apologize that you do not feel you were represented fairly, but you were in court with me during all pre-trial hearings and numerous trial preparation meetings, and never once mentioned that you were unhappy with our representation. You informed us that you did not wish to go to trial since you were facing 10 years in prison, and represented to us that you were very satisfied with the reduction to a misdemeanor probation. (By the way, I believe you were smart and made the right decision in doing so.) I apologize if you do not feel this was satisfactory, but I believe you ultimately made the best decision given the facts, and I'm sorry that you are not more satisfied with our work. Please contact me at any time and I would be happy to sit down with you and have a conversation to discuss this in more detail. Thank you, and I wish you the best.

- Tony Ross

We are so thankful that we had Colleen Carboy on our side! After multiple attempts of trying to find an attorney who would help us after our elderly mother was physically assaulted, Colleen was the only one who was willing to meet with us. Her background as a nurse still shines through with the empathy and compassion she displayed toward us and especially with our mother. As for the attorney side of her, she fights for what is right and advocates for those who wouldn’t otherwise have a voice. She prepared us well for what to expect in the process and kept us in the loop every step of the way. We will forever be grateful to her for taking our case when nobody else would.

Colleen Carboy is a phenomenal champion for her clients.  She handled an egregious injury case involving our elderly mother and a well known, very expensive memory care facility in the metroplex.  We had a positive outcome thanks to Ms. Carboy's tireless efforts and her vast knowledge of both the medical and legal professions.  With the perfect balance of pragmatism and fierce advocacy, our family was in very capable hands and we always felt like we had the best representation possible for our situation.  Deserves more than five stars - A++!

I highly recommend Mrs. Colleen Carboy (attorney) for anyone needing help for a medical/personal injury situation.  She helped me tremendously and successfully through a very difficult and traumatic experience.  She was professional, understanding, friendly, knowledgeable and responded quickly to my calls.  Her expertise and follow through lead to me winning my case and being able to put the matter behind me with positive resolve and justice.

Extremely disappointed in my experience. I took a legal issue to them and they continually told me they were working on it. After over a year passed by, they told me they decided not to take the case because it would be their experts testimony against the experts of the hospital where my wife died. I later contacted several other attorneys to request they look at the case, but they all told me there was not enough time because the two year statute of limitations was approaching.

Ms. Guzman was court appointed to a family member. She did not answer the phone for several weeks and did not call back. When I finally got through to her receptionist, she was short and rude. If you want someone who won't get you prepared for your divorce, who ignores your email, who does not care about your case or doing her job ! Muy mala licenciada, no te prepara y ignora tus emails.  mejor paguen un poco más y agarren a alguien que si quiera alludarlos!!!!

If she was appointed by the court to represent you, I'm sorry! She was my court appointed attorney on a total bogus assault charge in which I was not guilty of. She told me I shouldn't do that bc there would be old white men on the jury that would basically just go with what the police report (falsely) stated I kept telling her I would take it to trial.  Finally the DA said they would just give be a fine.  I agreed to that, and she told me that she was going to tell the judge to wave her fees , but come to find out she lied and now I have to pay over $1200 for her services. She did nothing for me and she lied! Do not recommend her at all! Her assistant is very unprofessional as well!

reviews.text:
Exceptional medical care, outstanding representation.

If your involved in a major accident and "think" you need a lawyer do not second guess yourself. From previous experience I knew that I wanted a litigator not a lawyer, there is a difference. If you do end up in court you want the GI Joe with the kung fu grip...not bozo the clown.

You'll easily find a billboard lawyer who will promise to save you a few dollars but complex cases take time and don't settle easily. Cutting corners to save a little can cost you tens of thousands of dollars on the back end.

Brian Mincher and Griffin Scheumack handled my case with an assist From "Izzy" (prelitigation) and Christina (litigation phase). The support staff was amazing and I can't say enough about Brian and Griffin. Be aware however that great litigators are forces of nature and the mindset required can be perceived as egotistical and opinionated. They did a great job of being forceful and blunt (which is their job) without running roughshod over me. In the end my case (like 95%+) settled at mediation. Without a doubt having highly respected litigators made a significant difference and allowed for a reasonable settlement.

Most of the time a review ends with something along the lines of "if I ever needed an attorney again" but in my case sadly I was rear ended at a stop light while the first case was ongoing so I can say (unfortunately) that I immediately DID hire them again.

I'd like to give a shout out to my medical "team". I received exceptional care from Epic Pain Management (Dr "G" and Dr Chen) as well as Dr Mott from Chiropractic Wellness Center...

All in all you certainly can find other options but I don't believe it's possible to find a better option in Collin County.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dave,
Thank you for sharing your detailed experience and for the kind words about Brian, Griffin, and our entire team. We’re glad to know you felt supported and confident throughout the process. Our firm is built on providing strong, results-driven representation while maintaining open communication and respect for our clients, and it’s rewarding to see that reflected in your experience. We also appreciate your continued trust in choosing us again. That confidence means a great deal to all of us.

Sincerely,
Jason K. Burress

To be honest, I was a little skeptical at first—because it’s rare, especially for a law firm, to have nothing but glowing reviews. Like many people, I wondered if the reviews were too good to be true. But after working with them personally, I can confidently say the reviews are absolutely genuine.

Burress Law truly lives up to its reputation. They are professional, thorough, and very effective. From the beginning, they made sure I had access to the right doctors and treatments, guiding me through the process with care and expertise. They handled every detail, and their negotiation skills secured a
strong settlement on my behalf.

While I haven’t yet received my settlement, I have complete trust in their process. It’s clear why this firm has earned such excellent feedback—they deliver results while treating their clients with fairness and integrity.

If you’re considering hiring Burress Law, take it from someone who was once skeptical: the five-star reviews are well-deserved.

PS this is a business email. I did not want my name posted publicly.

Man am I upset to be updating this. I've worked with Reib and Co. for years and thus far only had to connect with them infrequently to review some legal docs for hiring/etc. I once used them personally at an extra fee (of course) for something not-business related and was overall pleased with that as well.

Recently though, I found out that several things I believed I was paying for as part of their access plan (litigation and representation if issues arise from the forms they reviewed with me) were not at all included. As I inquired to see the details - admittedly a little shocked that several of the things told to me verbally years ago were not in the thing I'd been paying for; I was expecting an email stating a list of services included perhaps.... But I instead got an email stating: "We will go ahead and refund your most recent $x payment from August 15th, and your Access Plan will officially conclude on September 19th"

I guess the several thousand$/yr I've paid them for several years makes me a small client of theirs or something, but man..
I don't fault a company for growing over time (which they have), but I'd prefer not to be treated as expendable after learning I was misinformed about services I was paying for.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Andrew,

Thank you for your feedback and for being a long-time Access Member. We appreciate the trust you placed in REIBLAW over the years.

To clarify for others reading — the Access Plan is a proactive legal service designed for business owners who want ongoing counsel for contracts, structure, and strategy. Estate planning services (such as wills and trusts) have always been outside the scope of the Access Plan and are handled under a separate flat-fee Legacy Package, as outlined in the signed terms.

Once it became apparent that the services you were requesting — specifically new estate planning documents — were outside the scope of the Access Plan, we processed a refund of your most recent payment and kept your plan active for 30 additional days to allow for a smooth transition.

We wish you continued success and appreciate the opportunity we had to serve you and your business.

reviews.text:
Whether you need guidance with business law, asset protection, or wills and estate, Scott Reib is the attorney you need in your corner. From our first meeting, he explained everything to me and my wife in terms that we could easily understand. Scott and his team are always very responsive when we have questions, even outside our regular monthly meetings.
We have been clients of Reib Law for more than 4 years now. We initially learned of Scott from a family member. We really like the subscription billing structure that they offer over the traditional billing structures that most attorneys have in place. Knowing that we have an affordable set monthly fee makes it easier for us to plan our yearly budget without having the worry of one day incurring a large bill if we ever need to battle a lawsuit.
We recently encountered an issue with an investor who had purchased a rental property from us in Lewisville. We had maintained regular contact with him for almost a month. However, the buyer had not paid us for nearly four days, and several voicemails after we closed the sale. When we called Scott, we were told he was in a meeting. So we explained the situation to his staff and received a return call from him within 10 minutes. Faced with a situation we had never encountered before, we were unsure about what steps to take. Scott explained that the buyer was in breach of the contract because they had not paid us by the next business day. He provided us with precise guidance on what to say to the buyer and assured us that if the money was not received by the next day, he would personally intervene the following morning. Since the buyer did not answer the phone, we left a message using the exact words Scott had instructed us to use. To our pleasant surprise, we received our money by the end of the day.
The skill set and decades of experience that Scott and his team have to offer give us peace of mind. We cannot recommend them enough!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for sharing your experience! We're incredibly grateful for your trust and long-standing partnership over the past four years. It’s wonderful to hear that our subscription billing structure has been a valuable tool in helping you plan your budget with confidence.

We understand that situations like the one you encountered with the property buyer can be stressful, and we’re glad Scott and the team could provide the prompt and effective guidance you needed. Your story highlights exactly why we do what we do—to give our clients peace of mind and ensure they have the support they need, no matter what challenges arise.

Your recommendation means the world to us, and we look forward to continuing to serve you and your family for many more years to come!

reviews.text: Aubry Dameron is absolutely fantastic! She did mediation for me and my child’s father and it was easy and great communication, she helped us come to a mutual decision quickly and efficiently!
Springer and Lyle LLP - particularly Aubry and Stephanie handled my case quickly and smartly, and we won successfully. They made the process smooth and stress-free — highly recommend!
Excellent law firm. Low fees. They actually cared about us as people and not just a paycheck. They worked tirelessly on our case. Would highly recommend

I thankfully don’t have much need for a lawyer, but when the time came, I’m glad we found Springer and Lyle!  They were very caring and helpful. Usual turnaround on a correspondence was not usually longer than a couple of days. Mr. Lyle even came to the dedication for my son on September 17, 2024. The City of Denton named the day after my son, and he was honored for his courage, bravery and actions on the day of the automobile accident. Taylor was always very cheerful and ready to help even if it was to just sit and listen to me tell her my frustrations.

My family would definitely refer them if we come across anyone needing a personal injury lawyer.

Frank G. Lyle was extremely professional and knowledgeable, always going the extra mile to support us in our case.  Mr. Lyle even came to support my family when my grandson was honored by the Mayor in Denton for his bravery in our car accident.  I highly recommend him to anyone seeking legal assistance with a car accident.

Used Springer & Lyle services to help my Dad get a divorce from his 2nd wife. Daniel and Jackie were extremely professional, provided great service and kept both he and I informed of every step. Highly recommend!

Daniel Abasolo and Jackie McInerney of Springer-Lyle were great to work with to handle my divorce. Jackie kept me informed and advised me how we can achieve my desired result. I’m very pleased with my outcome and would definitely recommend this team to anyone looking for a winning team.

Only have the most positive things to say! Specifically about Daniel and Jackie. They both have been so much help and willing to answer all my questions about my divorce and custody agreement. Thank you guys so much!

This attorney truly stands out for their genuine care and support, especially when it comes to helping students. They take the time to listen, offer thoughtful guidance, and make legal matters easier to understand. What really makes a difference is their ability to communicate directly and respectfully, creating a comfortable environment for open discussion. Their dedication and professionalism are deeply appreciated.

Where to begin?
I could go on and on about how pleased and grateful I am for Frank and his team's help, but in the spirit of keeping things short and to the point: if you've suffered a personal injury that wasn't your fault—look no further. (Check out my wife’s review under RLR TX for more details.)

We interviewed 12 law firms and narrowed it down to three. My accident involved being run over by an 18-wheel rock hauler on a backcountry road. Two of our final three choices were actually cyclists themselves, so I assumed one of them would be the right fit. But after meeting Frank, my gut told me he was the one.
Frank is down-to-earth (a true Southern gentleman), honest, genuine, compassionate—and he has decades of experience successfully handling personal injury cases. He settled our case in a mind-blowingly short amount of time (just under five months).

I’ve included some photos of my bike to give you an idea of how serious the injuries were. There were many.

I am forever grateful to Frank and his team. Call his firm—you’ll understand exactly why I'm writing this. Wishing you all the best with your case and your recovery.

Working with Frank Lyle was truly a turning point for us. After my husband’s bicycle accident, we were completely overwhelmed. I spoke with 9 different law firms based on various recommendations, and Springer-Lyle was one of them. From the very beginning, Frank stood out. He took the time to really listen to our situation, clearly explained the legal process, and immediately made us feel supported. Out of all the firms I spoke with, Frank was the only one who came across as genuinely caring and sincere.

Some of the other firms insisted we come to their offices—which honestly felt more like a pitch to show off their expensive space than an effort to help us. Frank, on the other hand, offered to come to our home since my husband was too injured to travel. That gesture alone said everything. And when we eventually visited their office, we found it to be welcoming, down-to-earth, and refreshingly unpretentious.

Frank and his team were responsive, professional, and incredibly thorough throughout the entire process. They took care of all the legal complexities, allowing us to focus on healing. Thanks to their efficiency and dedication, my husband received a settlement in a surprisingly short amount of time.

What impressed me most was how much Frank truly cared—not just about the case, but about us as people. He checked in on my husband’s recovery and always made sure we felt informed and supported, both physically and emotionally. If you're looking for a personal injury attorney who’s not only smart and experienced but also genuinely compassionate, I can’t recommend Frank Lyle highly enough.

Thank you, Frank, for everything you did for our family!

Great office and team!

My husband was unexpectedly in a car accident that happened to turn into a long drawn out situation due to multiple medical procedures. We came across attorney Frank Lyle through a referral and it was such a relief! The whole process was very smooth, and everyone in the office was very helpful and answered every and all questions we had along the way. Taylor was amazing, and she was such a pleasure to work with! She put in so much work and it was very much appreciated. Although we hope to never be back in the same situation, but if we do, we would DEFINITELY choose to work with Springer & Lyle again! Honest, straightforward people that have your best interest at heart! Would absolutely recommend!

My wife and I had an opportunity to work with Aubry Dameron and Rhonda Dowling for estate planning. Attorney Aubry Dameron and her help Rhonda Dowling did a great job. They were very helpful and very professional, yet very friendly. It was a pleasant experience. We highly recommend Aubry Dameron for any future legal matter.

I appreciated Mr. Springer's and Rhonda's professionalism in addition to their understanding of our difficult time.  They were courteous, respectful, and informative about the negatives and positives of concluding our matter in a timely manner.  They are good people, I hope you consider them.

Being involved in an automobile accident is a horrible experience, but when the accident is caused by an underinsured motorist causing both property and bodily injury it magnifies that terrible experience. We needed legal help. We contact Frank Lyle at Springer and Lyle. Frank and Taylor were so knowledgeable, personable, and caring. They took care of everything which eliminated all of the stress and worry as a result of being involved in this accident. I am so thankful for all of the work, communication, and help that was provided to me and my family during this time. It was an exceptional experience at such a difficult time. I highly recommend this legal team.

My Sisters and I looked to Springer & Lyle to help us with the probate process of our Mother’s estate.  We were very appreciative with the way they listened, answered our questions, and made us feel comfortable.  It was the third time we have used them, all with more than positive results.  We would certainly recommend them to anyone in need of legal advice or representation.  Thank you for everything Jeff, Frank, and Rhonda.

Springer and Lyle, L.L.P. and all associates have been incredibly helpful! They literally took care of everything. We met once, a month after I had been released from the hospital and they kept in touch with me with every step taken to remedy my situation. I came out with zero medical bills, car paid off and a quite a bit more too. They always asked me how I felt before making a decision and offered advice when I wasn't sure. Hopefully nothing like this happens again to us or anyone else but if it does they have my full recommendation and support.  Thank you so much for doing right by me with all the hard work yall have put in. It can never be forgotten!

I had the opportunity to work with Aubry Dameron and Rhonda Dowling of Springer- Lyle throughout a very difficult guardianship case for my father. With the guidance and efforts of both Ms. Dameron and Ms. Dowling, the family was able to navigate a very complicated matter in a clear and concise fashion. We are so very thankful for their services and would highly recommend engaging them for any legal matter.

This firm is the best in the area.  So friendly and takes really good care of you.  Would highly recommend .  Always felt welcome and that they would take care of us..  I would give 10 stars if I could!
Fast and efficient!  Great communication!  Highly recommend!

Springer and Lyle handled my Divorce .  It was more complicated because my Spouse lives out of State and took almost a year to complete.  Daniel Abasolo was my Attorney and did an excellent job at the Arbitration session and in representing me.  His paralegal Jackie McInerney was outstanding answering all my questions and concerns, and in her follow-up with opposing counsel.  I give Springer and Lyle my highest recommendation and a very big Thank You for a job well done
I have worked with Jeff and Frank on cases in the past and I cannot say enough good things about them!
Wonderful representation. Very professional and handled our case with grace and care. Highly recommend!
Don't call....they have heavy work load & not accepting any cases!
reviews.ownerResponse: Our firm’s philosophy has always been to manage the volume of cases we handle in order to insure the quality of work on each file. While we would like to help everyone who contacts us, it is often not possible if we want to provide our clients with the assistance they deserve. Although you are not a client, it is very important to us that we provide excellent service. Please feel free to call us so we can address your concerns.

reviews.text:
I am a disabled veteran living out of state. I have been searching for legal representation in Denton County for close to a month!!! It took over a week for this outfit to return phone calls and emails. After my intake from someone named Jackie (who seemed to be a good listener), I received a call from one of the lawyers. While he spent 30 minutes talking to me, I had to point out the details of the summons and decision I received. He was making massive assumptions based, I assume, on his human processing experiences because that is exactly how it felt - just another human to process through the courts of Denton County.

The man I talked to had the audacity to inform me that 'lawyers cost money'?!!!!!!!! The fact that I am a disabled veteran has nothing to do with my ability to pay or UNDERSTAND that legal services cost money. He had flat affect, was monotone and it was like pulling teeth to get any information out of him. How can someone decide to hire an attorney without authentic communication?

Whoa to those who live in Denton County and need ethical, caring legal representation. I'm so glad I left that place!!!

Update:  I'm revising this review to a 3 because Mr. Lyle, one of the owners of the firm, took the time to contact me and apologize for the Associate Lawyer I spoke with......Mr. Lyle's demeanor, insight, and kindness was far and above what I had previously experienced. I'm very grateful.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Jacqueline, thank you for speaking with me about your initial conversation with one of my associate attorneys. I appreciate the feedback. We’ll strive to do better next time and I’m sorry that we could not represent you in the appeal of your case. I wish we would have had the opportunity to consult with you before the judgment was entered. Nevertheless, I hope the best for you and am glad we had the chance to speak. Frank Lyle

reviews.text: I was in an accident caused by another driver. I thought I would be able to handle the claims on my own, but the bills were large and complicated. I sought legal advice and found Springer Lyle in Denton. Frank Lyle and staff reviewed facts surrounding the incident, provided needed guidance and handled contact with insurance companies and other claimants. They were able to reduce the payment demands and settle insurance companies as well as recover additional funds for us. They handled all the details with these parties making it easy for us moving forward. We heartily endorse Springer Lyle to anyone needing legal assistance. They were professional and kind in understanding the situation and our needs.
Worked with Daniel and Jackie

I have unfortunately been in 2 wrecks that were very different from each other since moving to Texas and I have used Frank everytime. I know that frank is the experience I need to guide me in the right direction. My last case took a very long time due to th3 other party but Frank's team kept in contact, always was able to talk through any question or fear I had. I would recommend anyone to Frank and his team.
These guys are the best!  I am a fellow Denton lawyer and refer all my business their way and have received nothing but positive feedback.  Highly recommend.

Unfortunately, I met the fine people practicing at Springer and Lyle, L.L.P. under tragic circumstances beyond my control or the control of those close to me. With that being said, I am very honored to say that with the upmost respect, I was treated with the dignity and self-regard that, at the time, I didn't know was to be the standard that they set for their clients and how they would deal with such matters throughout. I did not have any experience with this type of law or the settlements that were to be sought but, that did not hinder them at all from explaining from the beginning how to go forth with claims, how to understand the process and how each step was to be dealt with. I was never "in the dark" about any phase of the legal process. In fact, I was ensured that any questions, concerns or hopes for my understanding would be answered and explained promptly and with full disclosure. I am still ignorant of the law and will probably continue to be but, with their help, I was given more than merely information but indeed benefits from their hard work than I could have hoped for or ever thought possible. I am and will remain in their debt. I would, without delay, recommend them for any future legal needs that I might see in the future for myself or my loved ones.

My Son was in a motorcycle crash and was in the hospital for 22 days.  Frank Lyle at Springer Lyle Attorneys represented us.  Their firm kept us in the loop during the entire process, from beginning to the final settlement.  Their firm is knowable in personal injury situations, directed us in what to expect, answered all our questions , and treated us with respect, understanding and compassion. We would highly recommend their firm.
Personal friendly and competent service. Highly recommended.

Frank truly took care of my family during a very difficult time.  He did so with integrity, kindness, honesty and expertise. He certainly went above and beyond. I am so grateful for him.  He and his team are such a pleasure to work with. I very highly recommend them!

When we needed help, Frank Lyle went so far above and beyond what we could have ever expected.  He and his team took care of so many details that we were not in a position to handle on our own. Frank and Taylor are supportive and compassionate people who helped us through a difficult time after the car accident. They got us far more than we expected and aggressively fought for us. Frank and his team know how to patiently persevere and win.

Mr. Lyle and Ms. Olson were extremely helpful after my accident. Their knowledge and negotiation skills made a tedious, difficult process much smoother. They were well-organized and great at communicating exactly where we were in the process and what I needed to do next.
Mr. Lyle was extremely responsive. Staff was friendly, professional, and knowledgeable. Mr. Lyle was able to handle the matter quicker than I was expecting.

Frank and Taylor were extremely efficient, prompt and helpful in dealing with the various entities and insurance companies required to settle our drunk driver accident claim. Frank’s expertise in this area was invaluable in navigating this process. There are many parts of this process that I didn’t even know existed that the insurance companies certainly wouldn’t clue you in to. The communication from Frank and Taylor was very satisfactory.
Frank and Taylor were prompt in handling my Daughter's case and kept me up to date and informed during the entire process. Highly recommend this Firm. Great outcome.

I was involved in a difficult case, Frank Lyle did not let that stop him. Frank and Jeff took on my case, they saw I needed help and choose to help me. From the very first conversation Frank was extremely kind, understanding, and informative! Frank made sure I knew and understood what was going on with my case at all times. He went above and beyond to answer any questions I had and gave me information about things I didnt know to ask about. Both Frank and Jeff are incredibly knowledgeable, friendly, and understanding! These men worked hard for me and always made sure I felt completely comfortable the entire time. They are wonderful! I would use them again and would recommend them to anyone! If I could give 10 stars I would!

Frank Lyle has given me excellent legal advice regarding an accident involving an 18 wheeler rear-ending me, which sent me to the ER and totaled my vehicle. Mr. Lyle helped me to understand the legal papers the trucking company want signed in order to settle their claim. After providing clarity of the legal jargon the trucking company was presenting to me, he then gave me advice on how to move forward and settle their claim in my best interest. He, also, informed me of legal rights I have pertaining to my own insurance company regarding PIP coverage, which they had not informed me of. He took an hour and a half of his time to patiently discuss the legalities of my circumstances at no cost. I highly recommend Springer and Lyle for your legal needs. I definitely consider Frank Lyle and his firm as my legal concierges today and in the future.

Eight months have passed since I wrote this review. Mr. Lyle has continued to spend numerous hours advising and representing me. He never wavered from his intent to obtain medical expenses I incurred from the accident on May 24, 2021. The billing was tangled up with auto/health insurance companies, along with third party billing firms. This was all sorted out because of Mr. Lyle’s tenacity, knowledge, and professionalism.  He, then, let the adjuster with the insurance company representing the trucking company know what was needed for a proper settlement. If I had not had Mr. Lyle and his firm backing me, this would not have been possible. I met with Mr. Lyle to sign the settlement and pay him for his legal services. He would not take any payment. I cannot say enough about Mr. Lyle. He is the most trustworthy, humble, and dedicated attorney I have ever met. I reiterate as I stated previously: I highly recommend Springer and Lyle for your legal needs. I definitely consider Frank Lyle and his firm as my legal concierges today and in the future.

When a family member was involved in an auto accident, we didn’t know what we didn’t know. From our first conversation with Frank Lyle we were put at ease. He was very empathetic to our situation and was very thorough in walking us through all of our options. He communicated with us regularly and guided us through the entire process until we were able to settle with the insurance company at a point that would have never been possible without his help. He is very knowledgeable, but does a great job of speaking in terms that anyone can understand. He took his time to listen and explained all of the options and possible outcomes of our situation. The best thing about Frank is he cares. Ultimately he wants to see that his clients are able to fully recover from whatever accident they had.

Integrity, if that is what you are in search of for your attorney, then your search is over! Frank Lyle and the entire staff was phenomenal. They are a staff of endearing people.and I am so happy they handled my case with care and diligence. They will not disappoint!

We came to Frank Lyle after my daughter had injuries as a result of being chased and bitten by a neighborhood dog that was unleashed resulting in a very expensive visit to the hospital along with downtime for recovery. We had a very difficult time contacting the owner of the dogs and knew it was time to get legal help. Frank and his team were a saving grace from day 1. I was very impressed right off the bat with how attentive and genuinely caring Frank was for my daughters recovery and overall well being. He made sure to get every little detail to build the case as well as get to know myself and my daughter to make sure he was the best voice to represent my daughter during this time. Our case resulted in a lawsuit that was settled, medical was paid as well as receiving compensation for my daughters pain and suffering.  Thank you Frank and assistant Debbie for making a stressful and traumatic situation so much easier for us!

I had a head on collision accident in February of this year. We were referred by a friend of ours. They took very good care of me. They were spot on everything that needed to be done. It went so smoothly and they always answered all of my questions and concerns. I don’t know what I would’ve done without them because there would’ve been no way for me or my husband to take care of this on our own. It was very stressful because of the injuries that I acquired and surgeries I had to have.  I want to say especially thanks to Frank and Deborah for everything that they did for me! Frank has been with us every step of the way to get our case resolved. He has a big heart and that shows through how he takes care of his clients. His knowledge, kindness and patience truly makes him the best in the business. His paralegal, Debbie, was also wonderful to work with. They make an amazing team. They were organized and thorough, always on top of anything that needed to be done. All of the office staff really went above and beyond to make sure we were always taken care of and welcomed. I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend Frank and his team to a friend, family member or anyone that needs help.

reviews.text:
He did not actually do anything I asked of him. He would not go over the discovery with me and show me the test results from a Hemp disposable that I bought from 360 Vapes here in Denton. It took him 3 months to get in contact with me, which was 2 weeks prior to my first pretrial plea date. He told me he would look over the discovery and we would go over it when he saw me two weeks later. When I got to the courthouse he was throwing a 2 year sentence at me, He said the 2 year sentence was a good deal because I had a background to which I told him I'd fire him and take it to trial first. This of course upset him and he said I wasn't giving him a chance to do his job. He reset my court date and the proceeded to admit he hadn't even looked at the Discovery, and that he would look in to it between now and the next date. So, without even knowing the evidence against me he tried to have me cop a plea.
It took my mom getting on Grock and asking questions about my case to lead to the fact that it was admissible because I didn't knowingly possess or purchase the Delta 8 as THC Delta 9. Which im still not convinced that the lab results ever came in. I told him worse case I would sign for 6 months time served, and this was just so I can get out and get the bar association to investigate him and his practices. If you've been to denton county and you have any understanding of how the courts work then you know that everyone is being railroaded. I couldn't make bail to prove my innocence in the situation and had I taken it to trial it would have been another 2 years sitting in that he'll hole. I asked for the discovery and he is telling me that evidence against me is private. That doesn't make any sense.  So, I would not recommend this man to give any legal advice at all. And I will be working to have his license to practice revoked.

reviews.text:
“Eric Navarrete- The lawyer who saved my life and set a standard no one else has matched.”

Several years ago, when my ex made false allegations during a custody battle, I was completely blindsided. I didn’t have the resources, the knowledge, or even the faintest idea of how to fight it. It was terrifying—overnight, I couldn’t even see or speak to my daughter until the court date.

Because God works in mysterious ways, a door opened for me. Through a connection with my company’s attorney, I was referred to Eric Navarrete—introduced as “the best of the best.” And he didn't just deliver, he blew it out if the park!

Eric didn’t just take my case, he owned it. He walked into that courtroom and dismantled the lies piece by piece. My dad said it felt like watching a courtroom movie where the superhero attorney wins—except this was real life, and Eric was that attorney. The odds weren’t against me as a mom—I have always been a devoted parent. The odds were financial: my ex’s family had deep pockets and every incentive to try to bury me. Eric cut straight through that advantage with skill, strategy, and truth.

What also sets Eric apart is how he treats his clients outside the courtroom. Unlike so many attorneys, he somehow finds the extra hours in the day to check in, offer reassurance, and make sure you never feel left in the dark. Even brief conversations with him carry weight, because they remind you that you’re not just a case file—you’re a person he genuinely cares about. Those “small” gestures of support are just as impactful as his award-worthy performances in the courtroom. And honestly? He's not just a hero for every single client that he represents, but somehow manages to be the ultimate hero for his family- who obviously comes first but you would never ever know it through the dedication and care Eric provides you throughout your entire case.

Years after that initial case, my ex tried to pull the same thing and because Eric moved to Denton County, I couldn’t hire him a second time. I went with another highly reviewed attorney, and it was the opposite experience—disinterested, unprepared, missing deadlines, and unjustifiable amounts of money for NOTHING.Since then, every other experience I’ve had with attorneys has been limited, but equally disappointing. Family and friends have shared their own stories—some horrific and truly heartbreaking. It’s devastating how many people put their lives, their children, and their savings in a lawyer’s hands, only to be let down. I can say from firsthand experience that Eric is worth every penny. He’s not the cheapest option; he’s the attorney who gives your family, your future, and your money the advocacy they deserve.

I’ve told Eric over the years how much what he's done for me means to me, but I don’t think he’ll ever fully grasp it. Even when a Facebook memory pops up from that time, I still cry and thank God for him. My baby was so little when he stepped in, and he helped when he didn’t have to. With my personal beliefs, I see him as one reason I know God is real. He isn’t advertising himself as a “Christian attorney”—that’s not what this is about. It’s simply how I understand the impact he had on my life. Whatever your beliefs, the truth is the same: he is an extraordinary lawyer and a deeply good human being.

If you’re part of the unfortunate group who wakes up one day with your world turned upside down—despite all the effort, love, dedication, and contribution you’ve poured into everything—and you don’t even know how to breathe, much less where to begin, please trust me: do whatever you can to retain Eric Navarrete. He is the advocate you want in your corner.

Regardless of what you're able to do, I pray peace, love, protection, and victory over you and your family during this trial in your life- but most importantly I pray for the precious child you're fighting for. You are loved.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed review, Nikki. Your words about Eric's dedication both in and out of the courtroom are truly appreciated. We are honored to have made a positive impact during such a challenging time.

It is a fundamental principle of our practice that all legal fees are determined strictly by the scope and nature of the legal work performed and the specific complexities unique to each case, without exception.
Any suggestion that our charges are based on a client's financial means, rather than the diligent and professional work invested in their matter, is entirely inconsistent with our firm's unwavering ethical standards and how we operate. We are committed to transparency in our billing processes, and we encourage any client with specific questions about their invoice to reach out to us directly for a confidential discussion. We stand by the value and dedication provided in all our legal representation.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience during mediation was not as you had hoped. As a neutral mediator, Eric's role is to facilitate discussions and help both parties, along with their attorneys, work toward a resolution. It’s important to note that mediation does not result in decisions made by the mediator, but rather the final agreements are made by the parties themselves and their attorneys.

Mediation is often an alternative to court proceedings where decisions are entirely out of the parties' hands. While mediation offers more control and flexibility, the process can still feel challenging, particularly when emotions and complex issues are involved. We do want to emphasize that any agreement reached in mediation is documented in a Mediated Settlement Agreement, which is signed voluntarily by all parties, and each party is encouraged to consult their attorney before signing.

We appreciate your feedback and are committed to continuously improving the mediation experience for all parties involved.

When we were looking for an attorney there were so many to choose from that we didn't know where to start.  However, choosing the right one was crucial due to the nature of our case. With my sister's sudden passing, my 7-year-old niece was left in an unsafe environment. We knew time was of the essence and we needed someone who would act fast. We needed an honest, genuine, and trustworthy attorney. We wanted someone we could trust to put our best interests at heart. That's when a good friend referred us to Eric Navarrette.

We passed several attorney's offices on our drive to Denton from Springtown to meet Eric and his team because he came so highly recommended.  His office was beautiful, his staff was friendly and very professional. Once we sat down with Eric we were confident that he was the one we were looking for. Within less than a week of hiring Eric as our attorney, we were picking up our niece with a court order! I felt so confident at every court appearance because he was right by our side representing us to the fullest. My sister's last wish was for us to raise her daughter, and with the help of Eric and his team, we will be adopting her!

I thank God every day for that referral and for placing Eric and his team in our lives when we needed them most. I am and will FOREVER be grateful for all he's done for our family, and for fighting so hard for my niece and her future!!  I highly recommend Eric and his team to anyone who needs a great attorney!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your story, Olivia. We are deeply honored that Eric and the team could act quickly to help your niece during such a difficult time. Your trust in us, and the referral that brought you to our office, means the world. Wishing you and your family all the best with the adoption.

My rating was not in error. Here’s your comment:

Eric Navarette mislead my fiancé in mediation. His ex-wife moved the the kids from Texas to Missouri. My fiancé made it very clear in mediation that if it wasn’t written in the agreement that she has to drive them to the airport that she would use that as an excuse to make us drive 40hours in a weekend to see the kids. Eric told my fiancé it wasn’t necessary to have it listed because it was part of “standard language.” Well sure enough she’s refusing to take them to the airport because it’s not written in the agreement. When my fiancé tried to contact Mr. Navarrette regarding this issue it took him about a week to respond AND he told my fiancé he couldn’t discuss this issue unless he pays to come back for arbitration. This is completely ridiculous when we already paid $900 to get screwed in mediation once. Thank you Eric for aiding in the suffrage of my family (including my 3 year old daughter). You’re a gem. **funny it took you almost a week to respond to an email but only an hour to respond to a bad review
reviews.ownerResponse: In mediations, the mediator is a third-party that is bound to remain neutral in the matter. As a mediator, Mr. Navarrette does not give legal advise to either party. It is the responsibility of the attorney representing the client to advise on language in an agreement between the two parties before it is signed, or for a pro se party to rely on their own knowledge and understanding. When Mr. Navarrette is a mediator and a settlement is reached, he is limited by the terms of the mediated settlement agreement and rules of professional conduct regarding communicating with the parties in a case he has mediated. If he was slow to respond, it was not meant out of disrespect to your fiancé, he is limited as stated above.

reviews.text:
I was really disappointed with my experience. The fees were much higher than what other attorneys quoted me for the same type of case. I was also encouraged to use a specific drug and alcohol counselor whose rates were about five times higher than other options I found, which didn’t feel right to me.

During my case, I felt there were mistakes or miscommunications in court, and only afterward did I start getting calls explaining what happened. The whole situation left me feeling uneasy and unsupported. Based on my experience, I wouldn’t hire this attorney again — in my opinion, you could get the same or better outcome with a less expensive attorney or even a public defender.

I didn't know it at the time I called in to the Tim Powers office, but my case was not the sort of thing these attorneys handled. In fact, I knew very little about my case because it happened 15 years ago while I was moving out of the state. I called several government offices and they wouldn't give me any useful information in regards to what I owed or needed to do to get my record clean. When I called this office, I was at my wits' end, calling any attorney whose number I could find in Denton County.

I was connected with Nicholas Ford. The first thing he did was tell me there was nothing their office could do to help me. The second thing he did was help me more than the previous 3 hours I'd spent searching govt sites and calling court buildings. Seriously, he was like, "Yeah, sorry, we don't handle that kind of case at this firm, but let me just see what I can find real quick..."  In 19 minutes this man took me from near-complete uncertainty about my situation to a clear game plan. Just outta the kindness of his heart. Unbelievably kind and professional, but the human-to-human connection, the altruism, that's what really shined through. Dude could've just hung up on me, you know?

I almost want to move back to Texas and do some crime so I can hire these guys, but instead I'll settle for leaving this review and recommending them to my friends.

If your reading the reviews to help you decide whether this would be a good attorney/law firm to hire for any legal assistance, I can tell you from my own experience it is NOT. Tim will tell you what you want to hear and make everything sound good, but doesn't fall through with it. . $5000 for 2 cases (class A and B theft) and didn’t even deliver. Save your money, because a court appointed attorney would probably work harder to get you a way better deal. I had the worst experience with his attorney Halle Brown, who was rude, unprofessional and put very little or barely any effort on my case. Every time i spoke with her it was an unpleasant conversation even over the phone, she would constantly speak over me, cut me off, gave me attitude and made faces like she seemed bothered and upset to represent me at every court appearance. Right away she wanted me to take the first offer (18 months probation) on the my first court appearance without even trying to dismiss it, lower it or even study my case to defend me because she didn’t want to “waste time starting probation” then lied and said i had been the one who said that when i complained. And when i complained  about her work ethics and  requested a different attorney from their law firm to represent me i was told by the secretary’s that they couldn’t do that, it would have to be Halle or i would have to hire another attorney and when i asked if they would be refunding me my money they changed the rates from a flat rate of $5000 to an hourly rate and said they would charge me by the hour, add it and then let me know what I would still owe them. I hired them back in September of 2025 called for weeks asking to speak to her to get information on my case because i did not know anything, and i didn’t get to speak to her until a week before I had my first court appearance, in the beginning of December and when I finally spoke with her i asked her basic questions about her experience, and my case and all she could tell me was that she didn’t know. Literally i dont know, i dont know, i dont know is what i kept hearing. You hire an attorney to handle these situations for you and give you some peace of mind but through the whole process i was only more stressed out. And to top it off they also just recently tried to bill me $7500 for each case (x2) for a trial that i never agreed in paying once they informed me it would be additional. Save your time and your money, not worth it.

My case is still an active case pending to close 1/16. Please if your care about your future use another law firm. I have 3 children, 1 on the way, 33 years old, no criminal record and had an incident where I’m being charged with state felony for a DWI with a child under 15YO. The most craziest part is my alcohol level was at .06. & Becky the associate who goes in there and represents you and supposedly fights for you basically said. The judge can prove you were intoxicated with examination if you take this to trial. Because lord knows I was going to OR you can plea guilty for state felony. (Mind you in this moment I knew nothing about the legal system) this is my first offense. I should have hired a lawyer that would have direct contact with the judge and not some associate. Please know you can hire council (another lawyer if you do not like where things are going) 3 years of my life to literally end up in the same scenario if I had hired Tim powers or NOT. Mind you, I have a breathalyzer in my car for 3 years now with no violations. The biggest factor what happens if I get fired from my job one day and they re run my background? EVERYONE knows it is so difficult for a felon to get a job. I am the sole provider to my children, 33 years old!!!!! & you are going to charge me with a STATE FELONY?! my children will end up on government assistance as will I due to this if something in my life changes. The fact that she or TIM could care less about this massive charge on someone is wild to me. Please go to someone else. 15k paid the same damn day was not worth the 3 years of just to be charged with a STATE FELONY.

I used Tim Powers when I had a incident happen in Denton which involved me getting a Felony and a few misdemeanors. reviews.text:
Through an unfortunate series of life events, I found myself in an untenable financial situation which necessitated defense against creditors at the door. And they can be brutal. The situation required compassion and tenacity, which I found in this law practice.

The team is great! Fantastic, really. But I must specifically highlight Kylie; she demonstrates true kindness, compassion, understanding, and a dedication to her work that is second to none. To her, I was a real person with real issues and real fears, not just a case. She spent endless time walking me through potential outcomes, answering my questions, and allowing me to vent when I needed. From my perspective, this was not just a job to her but rather a calling—to help people that find themselves in hard times and offer them hope. I never felt as if I was being dismissed or minimized, and I can’t tell you how meaningful that was to me. She is the BEST, and the firm best keep her forever because she is a part of what makes this a solid team. She is integral and an amazing human being.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for your heartfelt and appreciative feedback. We're deeply grateful to hear that our team, and Kylie in particular, were able to provide the compassion, understanding, and support you needed during a very difficult time. It means the world to us to know that we made a meaningful difference.

Please know that we are here for you and that our doors will always be open should you need our assistance again in the future. Thank you again for taking the time to share this appreciation. It means more to us than you could know.

reviews.text: Very professional as well as extremely friendly service. Everyone I had communication with was super understanding and helpful, from my initial consult all the way through the completion of my case. This firm made one of the most stressful life situations, an easy and pleasant time. I can't thank them enough and this review can't capture how pleased I am to have had them represent me.
